name: Eva Taylor
abstract: Eva Taylor (January 22, 1895 — October 31, 1977) was an American blues singer and stage actress.

concerts.label: Music Temple of the World Symphony Orchestra

about_quotes.speaker: George "Pops" Foster
about_quotes.evidence: INTERVIEWER: ...I heard, though, that Eva Taylor, his wife, had left him or something. [INTERVIEWEE] No, she, she, oh, she didn't have been separated a long time.

about_quotes.speaker: Pug Horton
about_quotes.evidence: I loved Eva Taylor’s singing. Because Eva had a great sort of clarity to her voice. So I was impressed by these singers.
about_quotes.doc_title: Pug Horton interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 22, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Wooding
about_quotes.evidence: On this bill was -- there was an act that's very good, Clarence Williams was there with Eva Taylor. That was his wife.

about_quotes.speaker: Dominic James "Nick" La Rocca
about_quotes.evidence: He was a talent scout for the Columbia where he and his wife, Eva Taylor, made recordings for Columbia.

about_quotes.speaker: Lizzie Miles
about_quotes.evidence: Eva Taylor Eva Taylor she done quite a bit of records for him and with him
